title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa discusses building and scaling AI Agents in production across thousands of government organizations. The talk covers agent loop architecture, A2A protocol, Effect-TS implementation, evaluation frameworks, long-context handling, monitoring, tool building, and integration with Claude and Cursor for workflow acceleration.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov details how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent deployed in production across thousands of government organizations. The talk walks through the agent loop architecture, the A2A protocol, and an Effect-TS implementation, along with evaluation frameworks, long-context handling, monitoring, and tool building. It also covers integration with Claude and Cursor to accelerate development workflows. The focus is on the engineering practices, observability, and evals required to run agents reliably at scale rather than on prototypes.
